facts.title: Chunked prefill logic
facts.fact_full: When `enable_chunked_prefill=True`, long prompts are split across multiple steps. The scheduler caps `num_new_tokens` at `token_budget` for waiting requests. Without chunked prefill, a request that can't fully fit in the budget is deferred. Key code: `scheduler.py:L682-L692` (chunked prefill decision) and `L684-L690` (when chunked prefill is disabled, skip requests that don't fit).

facts.title: FCFS scheduling order
facts.fact_full: vLLM uses First-Come-First-Served (FCFS) by default. The running list order = arrival order. The LAST request in `self.running` is the lowest priority for preemption. This is why `self.running.pop()` preempts the lowest-priority request. For testers: when testing preemption, the request added LAST should be preempted first.

facts.title: Config file path — no /v1/ directory
facts.fact_full: Scheduler config is at `vllm/config/scheduler.py`, NOT `vllm/v1/config/scheduler.py`. The `/v1/` prefix applies to `core/sched/` (scheduler.py, output.py) and `request.py`, but NOT to the `config/` directory. Key defaults in config/scheduler.py: L56 max_num_scheduled_tokens=None (computed at runtime), L63 max_num_seqs=DEFAULT_MAX_NUM_SEQS, L84 enable_chunked_prefill=True. For writers/reviewers: always verify source paths against the actual repo. The source grounding linter only checks implementation REFERENCE comments and source mapping rows, not narrative prose paths.

facts.title: scheduler.py at commit 98661fe — verified line ranges
facts.fact_full: At commit `98661fe` (current pinned source), canonical line ranges: Scheduler.__init__ L67-L176; schedule() L352-L945; _preempt_request() L952-L972; _update_after_schedule() L974-L998; update_from_output() L1290-L1551; add_request() L1728-L1748; _free_request() L1813-L1834. Re-verify if `git rev-parse HEAD` in `instances/vllm/source/` differs from `98661fe`.

facts.title: Phase 2 has TWO guards: `not preempted_reqs` AND `pause_state == UNPAUSED`
facts.fact_full: scheduler.py:L568 reads `if not preempted_reqs and self._pause_state == PauseState.UNPAUSED:`. The pause-state piece supports the engine's `PauseState.PAUSED_ALL` freeze. If a tester ever needs to model engine pause/resume, the gate must be reintroduced; otherwise paused engines admit waiting requests, violating pause semantics.
